description: Support N labeled ICS calendar subscriptions (replacing the two fixed googleIcsUrl/outlookIcsUrl fields), plus a selfEmails list so users with multiple email aliases aren't misidentified as attendees of their own meetings. Migration from the existing single-URL config runs once, automatically.

userStories.title: Extend config with calendars[] and selfEmails[] + one-time migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the config to hold an arbitrary list of calendar subscriptions and a list of self-email aliases, migrating from the existing single-URL fields without data loss.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend Config interface in src/main/config.ts with: calendars: Array<{id:string, label:string, provider:'google'|'outlook'|'ics', url:string, enabled:boolean}> and selfEmails: string[]
Defaults for both are empty arrays
Keep existing googleIcsUrl and outlookIcsUrl fields in the interface (do not delete) — treat as deprecated, only migration reads them

Add a one-time migration helper callable from main.ts on app start: if calendars.length === 0 AND (googleIcsUrl OR outlookIcsUrl is non-empty), push one calendar row per non-empty URL with crypto.randomUUID() id, label 'Google'/'Outlook', correct provider, enabled:true
Migration is idempotent: running twice produces the same result (guard on calendars.length === 0)
Add helpers in a new src/main/calendar-config.ts (or extend config.ts): addCalendar(row without id), updateCalendar(id, patch), removeCalendar(id), listCalendars()
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Extended Config in src/main/config.ts with calendars[] and selfEmails[] (defaults []); kept legacy googleIcsUrl/outlookIcsUrl (marked @deprecated). Added CalendarSubscription/CalendarProvider exports plus listCalendars/addCalendar/updateCalendar/removeCalendar/setSelfEmails helpers. migrateLegacyCalendars() seeds one row per non-empty legacy URL on first run, guarded by calendars.length === 0 (idempotent). Called from app.whenReady() in main.ts with info log when a migration actually happens. Both tsc --noEmit passes (main + renderer). Project has no test runner configured, so 'tests pass' is vacuously satisfied.

userStories.title: calendar-watcher polls all enabled calendars and merges events
userStories.description: As a user, when I have multiple calendars enabled, I want events from all of them to appear in my upcoming meeting list, de-duplicated.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/main/calendar-watcher.ts, replace the direct reads of config.googleIcsUrl and config.outlookIcsUrl with iteration over getConfig().calendars.filter(c => c.enabled)
Fetch all enabled calendars in parallel via Promise.allSettled — one failing calendar does not block the others
Merge events from all calendars into a single list; de-duplicate by composite key (event.id + event.startTime.toISOString())
On duplicates, prefer the entry with more attendees; if tied, first-fetched wins
Tag each merged event with a new field sourceCalendarId: string referencing the calendar id it came from
Public API unchanged externally: getUpcomingEvents(), on('meeting-starting'), on('meeting-reminder') keep same shape
Log per calendar per poll: 'calendar-watcher:fetch | calendarId=X label="Y" got=N events'
Aggregate poll summary: 'calendar-watcher:poll | Done — total=M unique events across K calendars'
Empty calendars array → skip polling with a debug log; no error
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Replaced googleIcsUrl/outlookIcsUrl reads in calendar-watcher.ts with iteration over getConfig().calendars filtered by enabled+non-empty URL. Fetches use Promise.allSettled so one failing calendar doesn't block the others. Each event is tagged with sourceCalendarId. Dedup uses composite key id+startTime.toISOString() and prefers the entry with more attendees (first-fetched wins on tie). Per-calendar log: 'calendar-watcher:fetch | calendarId=X label="Y" got=N events'. Aggregate poll log: 'calendar-watcher:poll | Done — total=M unique events across K calendars'. Empty-enabled-list path skips with an info log (logger.ts has no 'debug' level). CalendarHealth keeps googleConfigured/outlookConfigured (now derived from calendars[]) so the existing Settings.tsx still compiles — US-004 will redesign that surface. Notifications fire from the deduped list to avoid double-notifying when the same event is on two synced calendars. Both tsc --noEmit passes (main + renderer). No test runner configured.

userStories.title: Centralize attendee self-filtering via isSelf(attendee) using selfEmails
userStories.description: As a user with multiple email addresses, I want the app to recognize all my aliases so I am not listed as an attendee of my own meetings and voice enrollment filters me out correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/main/self-identity.ts exporting isSelf(attendee: string): boolean
isSelf reads getConfig().selfEmails and getConfig().userName
Returns true if normalized lowercase attendee contains any entry from selfEmails, OR if userName is non-empty AND attendee.toLowerCase() includes userName.toLowerCase()
Handles attendee formats: plain email, plain display name, 'Name <email>' combined
When both selfEmails and userName are empty, returns false (preserves current fresh-install behavior)
Replace inline attendee self-filter at src/main/main.ts:162 (in replaceSpeakerLabels) and src/main/main.ts:210-218 (in autoEnrollVoices) with isSelf(a)
Replace the equivalent inline filter at src/main/database.ts:395, :554, :565 with isSelf
Add unit test file: test email-only match, test display-name fallback match, test 'Name <email>' format, test mixed-case, test empty-config returns false
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added src/main/self-identity.ts exporting isSelf(attendee) which reads getConfig().selfEmails and getConfig().userName, plus a pure matchesSelf(attendee, selfEmails, userName) helper used for unit tests (no electron-store dep). Rule: lowercased attendee.includes() any non-empty selfEmail, OR attendee.includes() trimmed non-empty userName. Handles 'Name <email>', plain email, plain display name, and mixed case; empty config returns false. Replaced inline filters: main.ts replaceSpeakerLabels (otherAttendees) and autoEnrollVoices (otherAttendees) → attendees.filter(a => !isSelf(a)); database.ts getPerson action-item filter → isSelf(owner) in the OR chain (dropped the local currentUserName var); database.ts getSuggestedPeople → replaced 'key === userName' equality check with 'if (isSelf(attendee)) continue' and moved the check above the lowercase key so self aliases are skipped before the frequency map is touched. Added src/main/self-identity.test.ts with node:assert/strict covering email-only, display-name fallback, 'Name <email>' combined, mixed-case, multi-email, no-match, empty-config, whitespace-only, and empty-attendee cases — verified green by compiling to a temp dir and running with node. Both tsc --noEmit targets pass.

userStories.title: Settings UI: calendar list with add/edit/remove/test/toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Settings page section where I can add, rename, disable, test, and remove calendar subscriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the two fixed IcsField components in src/renderer/Settings.tsx with a CalendarList component
Each row renders: label input, provider dropdown ('Google'|'Outlook'|'Other'), URL input, enabled toggle switch, 'Test' button, 'Delete' button
'Test' button invokes existing inwiseAPI.testCalendarUrl and shows result inline (connected + event count, or error)
'+ Add calendar' button at bottom appends a new empty row; label input is auto-focused
Changes persist via new IPC methods: inwiseAPI.addCalendar(row), updateCalendar(id, patch), removeCalendar(id) — add corresponding ipcMain handlers and preload bridge entries
Debounced on-change save (500ms) or save-on-blur for text fields; toggle saves immediately
After migration (US-001), user sees one pre-populated row for each of their old googleIcsUrl/outlookIcsUrl
Empty state: 'Add your first calendar to get started — paste a secret ICS link from your calendar settings'
CalendarStatus component at top aggregates across all enabled calendars: total event count + any-failing flag
Toggling a calendar off and waiting one poll cycle: events from that calendar disappear from the upcoming list
Typecheck passes

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open Settings, add a calendar with an invalid URL, confirm error inline; add one with a valid test URL, confirm event count shown; toggle off and confirm removal from upcoming list
userStories.notes: Replaced the two fixed IcsField components in src/renderer/Settings.tsx with a new CalendarList component + CalendarRowView sub-component. Each row renders: label input (debounced save 500ms, save-on-blur), provider dropdown (Google/Outlook/Other → 'google'/'outlook'/'ics'), URL input (same debounced+on-blur save pattern), Enabled checkbox (saves immediately via onPatch), Test button (wires to existing testCalendarUrl), Delete button (with confirm dialog). '+ Add calendar' appends a new empty row via addCalendar and auto-focuses the label input on the new row. Empty state: dashed-border placeholder with 'Add your first calendar to get started — paste a secret ICS link from your calendar settings.' CalendarStatus component unchanged — already aggregates across all enabled calendars via the health object's status/eventCount/lastError. Added three new IPC handlers in main.ts — calendar:add, calendar:update, calendar:remove (plus calendar:list) — each logs and calls calendarWatcher.refresh() so toggling a calendar off removes its events from the next upcoming list without waiting for the 5-minute interval. Added a public refresh() method on CalendarWatcher that wraps poll(). Preload bridge exposes listCalendars/addCalendar/updateCalendar/removeCalendar. The renderer Config interface no longer references googleIcsUrl/outlookIcsUrl (still in electron-store for backwards-compat/audit, only migration reads them). After migration (US-001), users see one pre-populated row per legacy URL because migrateLegacyCalendars() seeded calendars[] during app start. Both main and renderer tsc --noEmit pass; webpack renderer build completes successfully. Electron app — dev-browser skill not applicable (renderer runs inside BrowserWindow, not a web browser).

userStories.title: Settings UI: selfEmails chip-input editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want to list all my email addresses so the app recognizes me across calendars.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Your email addresses' section in src/renderer/Settings.tsx, near the existing userName field
Render existing selfEmails as removable chips; each chip has a small '×' button to remove
Text input accepts email; pressing Enter or comma adds it as a chip and clears the input
Validate on add with a basic email regex (e.g. /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/); invalid entries show inline error for 3 seconds and are not added
Duplicates are rejected silently (no-op if email already present, case-insensitive)
Persist via new IPC inwiseAPI.setSelfEmails(list: string[]) — add ipcMain handler + preload bridge entry
Initial value read from existing inwiseAPI.getConfig() shape
Helper text: 'Separate from display name. Used to identify you in attendee lists across multiple calendars.'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: add an email, reload the page, confirm chip persists; try an invalid entry, confirm rejection; remove a chip, confirm removal persists
userStories.notes: Added SelfEmailsEditor component in src/renderer/Settings.tsx — rendered in the Voice Enrollment section right after the 'Your Name' input. Chips display each email with a × remove button; Enter or comma commits the draft; a trailing-comma in the input also auto-commits. Validation uses /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/ — invalid entries show an inline red error for exactly 3 seconds (setTimeout cleared on unmount + on the next error to prevent flicker). Duplicates are rejected silently with case-insensitive comparison. Backspace on an empty draft removes the last chip (standard chip-input ergonomic). Persistence: each mutation calls the new inwiseAPI.setSelfEmails(next) bridge, which invokes 'config:setSelfEmails' in main.ts — the handler trims and drops empty strings before calling setSelfEmails() from config.ts, then logs the resulting count. Added selfEmails: string[] to the renderer Config interface so the whole-config save path (Save Settings button) also persists the array. Initial value read via existing inwiseAPI.getConfig(). Helper text: 'Separate from display name. Used to identify you in attendee lists across multiple calendars.' Both tsc --noEmit targets pass; webpack renderer build succeeds. Electron renderer — dev-browser skill not applicable; typecheck + webpack build are the real gates.

userStories.title: Handle simultaneous meeting starts: conflict modal with 30s auto-select fallback
userStories.description: As a user with two meetings starting at the same moment, I want the app to tell me about the conflict and let me pick which one to record, instead of silently dropping one.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect conflict in src/main/main.ts meeting-starting handler: if isRecordingActive is true OR another meeting-starting fired within the last 90 seconds, treat as a conflict
On conflict, send IPC 'meeting:conflict' to main window with payload { active: Event, incoming: Event } — renderer shows a modal with title, start time, and attendee count for each, plus Pick buttons
If mainWindow is hidden/minimized or doesn't exist, fall back to a desktop Notification with actions (new Notification with { actions: [...] }).show()

If the user does not respond within 30 seconds, auto-select: score each meeting by the count of attendees matching selfEmails/userName (use isSelf helper from US-003); higher score wins; tiebreaker = earlier startTime; final tiebreaker = first-fired wins
Log the decision: log('info', 'calendar-watcher:conflict', `recording="X" passed-over="Y" reason=auto-selected|user-selected`)
The passed-over meeting still appears in getUpcomingEvents() and the upcoming list (no silent removal from the calendar state)
If only one meeting starts (no overlap), current single-overlay path is preserved — no modal, no delay
Add a renderer ConflictModal component in src/renderer/ (e.g. components/modal/MeetingConflictModal.tsx) rendered from the main App or Sidebar when meeting:conflict fires
Typecheck passes

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: trigger two synthetic meeting-starting events within 5 seconds of each other via a test IPC or dev shortcut; confirm modal appears, pick one, confirm only that one records and log shows the decision
